stem: The profit, in dollars, that a company receives from manufacturing q units of a product is given by a quadratic function P(q). The graph of y = P(q) in the qy-plane intersects the q-axis at q = 0 and q = p, where p is a positive constant. What does p represent in this context?

answerOptions.content: The maximum profit, in dollars, the company can earn from the product
The number of units that yields the maximum profit
The profit, in dollars, when the company produces 0 units
The number of units produced that results in a profit of $0

rationale:
Choice D is correct. A graph intersects the q-axis where y = 0, i.e., where the profit is $0. The intercept at q = 0 corresponds to producing nothing; the second intercept at q = p corresponds to a different production level that also yields $0 profit.

Choice A describes the maximum y-value (at the vertex), not an x-intercept. Choice B describes the q-coordinate of the vertex. Choice C describes the y-intercept itself.
